story.title: This humanoid robotics company is going public, but its CEO isn’t promising a robot in your home anytime soon
story.summary: While other humanoid startups chase sky-high valuations, Agility Robotics is betting its future on execution — and a SPAC.

points.heading: Agility Robotics Goes Public
points.detail: Agility Robotics will go public via a SPAC, valuing it at $2.5 billion and raising over $620 million, a robotics record.

points.heading: Targeting Warehouses, Factories
points.detail: Agility's Digit robot is designed for moving heavy objects in warehouses and factories, optimized for industrial spaces.

points.heading: Strong Revenue Pipeline
points.detail: Agility has over $300 million in booked, multi-year revenue for about 1,000 robots via a robots-as-a-service model.

points.heading: Safety Focus is Key
points.detail: Agility emphasizes industrial safety certification, having designed robots from the start to meet operational requirements in customer facilities.
